questions.question: Why is improvement in food resources necessary?
questions.answer: Improvement in food resources is necessary to increase food production to meet the needs of the growing population.

questions.question: Name two major sources of food.
questions.answer: Plants and animals are the two major sources of food.

questions.question: What are cereals? Give two examples.
questions.answer: Cereals are crops that provide carbohydrates. Examples: rice and wheat.

questions.question: What are pulses?
questions.answer: Pulses are crops that are rich in proteins.

questions.question: What are oil seeds? Give one example.
questions.answer: Oil seeds are crops grown to obtain edible oil. Example: mustard.

questions.question: What are Kharif crops? Give one example.
questions.answer: Kharif crops are grown during the rainy season. Example: paddy.

questions.question: What are Rabi crops? Give one example.
questions.answer: Rabi crops are grown during the winter season. Example: wheat.

questions.question: Define hybridisation.
questions.answer: Hybridisation is the crossing of genetically dissimilar plants to produce improved varieties.

questions.question: What is manure?
questions.answer: Manure is organic matter obtained from plant and animal waste used to improve soil fertility.

questions.question: What are fertilizers?
questions.answer: Fertilizers are chemical substances added to soil to supply nutrients to plants.

questions.question: What is irrigation?
questions.answer: Irrigation is the artificial supply of water to crops.

questions.question: Name two sources of irrigation.
questions.answer: Wells and canals are sources of irrigation.

questions.question: What are weeds?
questions.answer: Weeds are unwanted plants growing along with crops.

questions.question: Why are weeds harmful?
questions.answer: Weeds compete with crops for nutrients, water and sunlight.

questions.question: What are pesticides?
questions.answer: Pesticides are chemicals used to control pests and diseases in crops.

questions.question: What is animal husbandry?
questions.answer: Animal husbandry is the scientific management of livestock.

questions.question: What is cattle farming?
questions.answer: Cattle farming is the rearing of cattle for milk production and agricultural work.

questions.question: What is poultry farming?
questions.answer: Poultry farming is the raising of domestic birds for eggs and meat.

questions.question: What is fish farming?
questions.answer: Fish farming is the breeding and raising of fish in ponds or tanks.

questions.question: What is aquaculture?
questions.answer: Aquaculture is the farming of fish and other aquatic organisms.

questions.question: What is composite fish culture?
questions.answer: Composite fish culture is growing different species of fish together in one pond.

questions.question: What is bee-keeping?
questions.answer: Bee-keeping is the rearing of bees for honey and wax production.

questions.question: What is apiculture?
questions.answer: Apiculture is another name for bee-keeping.

questions.question: Name one product obtained from bee-keeping.
questions.answer: Honey is obtained from bee-keeping.

questions.question: What is mixed farming?
questions.answer: Mixed farming is the practice of growing crops and raising livestock together.
